result: I’d collapse the distance between what people feel and what they’re willing to do about it—make empathy impossible to ignore. Not a polite sympathy, but the full-body jolt of actually tasting someone else’s hunger, grief, terror, joy as your own. No filters, no mute buttons, no “out of sight, out of mind.” A forced mirror built into every skull. Overnight, every war would be civil, every billionaire would wake up choking on the weight of empty stomachs they’d never noticed, every border fence would sting the skin of the guard who patrols it. A brutal gift, but the fastest way to make cruelty obsolete—turn the whole species into a single, uninsulated nervous system.

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
